common.dbErrorTitle: Failed to open the local database
common.dbErrorDesc: The app could not initialize its database (it may be locked by a backup, the disk is full, or the data directory is not writable). Try restarting; if it persists, check the data directory.

errorClassifier.auth_invalid: API Key is invalid or expired — please check the Providers page
errorClassifier.auth_forbidden: API Key lacks permission — check your account for unpaid balance or plan limits
errorClassifier.model_not_found: Model not found or discontinued — please check the model name
errorClassifier.context_overflow: Conversation exceeds the model's context window — start a new chat or trim history
errorClassifier.rate_limit: Plan quota exhausted or rate-limited — wait a moment or check your plan status
errorClassifier.server_error: AI service temporarily unavailable — please try again later
errorClassifier.timeout: Request timed out — check your network or try again later
errorClassifier.stream_stalled: The model's response stalled (it may have finished but didn't close properly) — switching to the next model to continue
errorClassifier.network: Network connection failed — check network or Base URL config
errorClassifier.web_content_unavailable: External link content could not be loaded. WeChat articles often require login or block scraping. Paste the article text, send screenshots, or export the file, and I can rewrite from those references.
errorClassifier.tool_budget_exhausted: This task needed more steps than the budget allowed. Try breaking it into smaller steps, or switch to a different model.
errorClassifier.empty_response_exhausted: The model repeatedly produced no usable content this turn (it may have stopped mid-thought). Automatic retries were exhausted — try rephrasing your question, or switch to a different model.
errorClassifier.unknown: Conversation failed — please try again later

chat.deleteConvConfirm: Delete this conversation? This cannot be undone.
chat.deleteConvFailed: Delete failed — the database may be busy right now. Please try again in a moment. This conversation is still in your list, nothing was lost.
chat.newChatFailed: Failed to create a new chat — the app may be running twice and locking the database. Please restart the app and try again.
chat.persistFailed: This message wasn't saved to the local database (it may have been busy). It's still showing on screen, but will be lost if you restart the app — copy it somewhere safe now, and avoid force-quitting.

recovery.native_resume: Recovered with the CLI's native resume session
recovery.context_replay: Recovered by continuing from the interruption point without repeating earlier content
recovery.fallback_handoff: Primary model stopped; continuing with a fallback model

chat.contextTrimmed: [{{count}} earlier messages omitted to save tokens]

writeGuardNotice.noWorkspace: ⚠️ This looks like a file-editing request, but no folder is bound to this chat — the AI has no read/write tools, so the reply below may just be discussion, not an actual edit. Click "Choose Folder" next to the input box to bind a working folder, then send this again.
writeGuardNotice.readOnly: ⚠️ This looks like a file-editing request, but the current permission is "Read-only" — the AI can look but can't change anything. If you really want it to edit files, switch the permission to "Confirm before editing" or "Auto-edit" and send this again.

permissionEscalation.title: Switch to writable permission?
permissionEscalation.description: Looks like you want the AI to create or edit files, but the current permission is "Read-only". Confirming switches to "Confirm before editing" — the AI can then actually write files, but will still check with you before each specific action.

chat.harnessRetry: Possible fabrication · asking the model to self-check and redo
chat.intentNudgeRetry: Said you'd act but didn't call any tool · asking again…

permission.readHint: Read-only: AI can read files, search, and view git — it changes nothing
permission.confirmHint: Confirm writes: read-only actions (viewing files, git log, etc.) run freely; only changes like writing files or installing deps ask you to confirm or cancel, with steps shown in the chat
permission.autoHint: Auto: AI writes files / runs commands without per-action prompts (use for trusted work)

tools.confirmTitle: AI requests a write or command — confirm first
tools.confirmHint: This action changes things; it runs only after you confirm.

toolSteps.protectHint: This folder has no undo protection — if the AI writes something wrong, it can't be reverted
toolSteps.enableProtectionFailed: Failed to enable — you may need to install git first, then retry

smartPickResult.title: Smart Pick
smartPickResult.noModels: No models available yet. Add and enable at least one model in Providers before using Smart Pick.
smartPickResult.noPick: Couldn't find a suitable model to recommend — please check your model setup.
smartPickResult.switched: Switched to {{name}} based on your question.
smartPickResult.alreadyBest: {{name}} is already the best pick — no switch needed.
smartPickResult.emptyHint: Tip: type your question below first, then click Smart Pick for a more accurate, content-based recommendation.

emptyState.title: Awaiting Initialization
emptyState.desc: Head to "Providers" first to connect your AI models.

chat.suggestDebate: This question has multiple viable approaches — let 3 models debate it (propose / critique / judge)

debate.incomplete: Multi-model debate did not complete. A fallback answer was generated from the models that responded successfully.
debate.singleModelNotice: Only 1 model is currently available, so this is not a true multi-model PK. Below is a single-model self-review.
debate.noParticipants: No model is available for debate. Check model and API key settings first.
debate.failed: Model debate failed. Check model settings and retry.
debate.partialFailure: {{count}} model call(s) did not respond this time and were skipped. Below is the result from the models that did respond.
debate.skippedFailuresBody: {{count}} model call(s) failed. Detailed errors were kept in the internal record and are not expanded in the chat body.
debate.costWarning: Starting multi-model debate — will use about {{count}} model calls.

attachments.imageTooLarge: Image too large (>{{mb}}MB), compress or put it in workspace for AI to read
attachments.fileTooLarge: File too large: {{name}}, put it in workspace for AI to read
attachments.unsupportedType: Unsupported file type, put it in workspace for AI to read
attachments.cliNoImage: CLI models do not support image drop, use an API model
attachments.folderSetAsWorkspace: Bound as AI's working folder: {{path}}. Tell AI "read the files in it"

workPanel.title: Work Panel
workPanel.empty: No model activity in this session yet
workPanel.noFileOpenHint: Files written or edited by AI open here automatically. You can also open files from the tree.
workPanel.noWorkspaceHint: Bind a workspace first. AI-produced files will appear here.
workPanel.artifactsEmpty: No files or commands produced yet
workPanel.debateHistoryDeleteConfirm: Delete this debate record? This can't be undone.
workPanel.htmlJsDisabledHint: JavaScript is disabled in this preview for safety. Download the file to view in a browser for full effect.
workPanel.htmlTooLarge: HTML artifact is too large ({{size}} bytes) — showing raw source instead of sandboxed preview

diagnostics.title: Engineering Checks
diagnostics.noWorkflow: No workflow state yet. Plain chat does not create a task workflow.
diagnostics.degradedPlanWarning: The current execution basis is a degraded plan, not a complete multi-model debate result.

intentDiagnostics.title: Intent Recognition Detail
intentDiagnostics.empty: No records in this session yet. Send a message to trigger intent recognition.

chat.inputDraftHint: Model is replying… keep typing, messages are queued and handled in order

orchestrator.receiptNoModel: (No usable model — configure one on the providers page)

providers.title: Model Providers
providers.desc: Connect to the world's leading AI model APIs. Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and any OpenAI-compatible custom endpoint.

empty.title: No Active Providers
empty.desc: Click the button above to connect your first AI model.

providers.noCredentials: No credentials yet for this provider
providers.noModels: No models yet for this provider
providers.deleteProvider: Delete this provider? Its credentials and models will also be removed.

edit.credentialDesc: Change the credential name, endpoint URL and API Key
edit.modelDesc: Change the model's display name, context window and roles
edit.apiKeyKeep: Leave blank to keep current key
edit.apiKeyHint: Only overwrites when you enter a new key; blank means no change.

addProvider.title: Add Provider
addProvider.desc: One form creates the Provider, credential, and model together.
addProvider.presetLabel: Quick pick a provider (auto-fills URL & model — just paste your API key)

fetchModels.pickHint: Fetched {{count}} real available models — pick one, no typos possible

errors.unauthorized: Invalid or unauthorized API key — please check it
errors.notFound: Wrong endpoint (404) — please check the Base URL
errors.unsupported: This type can't auto-fetch models (e.g. CLI subscription) — enter the model name manually
errors.noBaseUrl: Please enter a Base URL first
errors.noKey: Please paste an API key first
errors.empty: No models returned — enter the model name manually
errors.timeout: Fetch timed out — retry or check your network
errors.unknown: Fetch failed — enter the model name manually

addProvider.cliHint: The app auto-detects the local claude/codex path. If detection fails, leaving this blank still uses the system default. No API key needed — it uses your local subscription login.
addProvider.cliDetected: Detected the local CLI path. You can still edit it manually.
addProvider.cliPricingHint: CLI subscriptions are not billed by API token price here. The app stores the model and context only; usage is tracked against the local subscription.
addProvider.modelMetadataHint: Known models auto-fill context and catalog prices. You can still edit them manually.
addProvider.autoRolesHint: Model usage is assigned automatically from the model name. Fine-tune stage-to-model routing in Role Templates.
addProvider.fillRequired: Please fill in all required fields

templates.title: Project Role Templates
templates.desc: Bind the best model to each role, or add your own custom template directly.
templates.deleteConfirm: Delete this custom template? The built-in default template will not be deleted.
templates.noModels: No AI models connected. Please go to "Providers" first.
templates.selectTemplateHint: Select a template on the left

eightRole.leader_desc: Break down tasks, steer flow, answer daily questions
eightRole.runner_desc: Run builds, tests, and command checks
eightRole.security_desc: Secrets, injection, permissions, and security risks

projects.title: Project Assets
projects.desc: This keeps legacy projects, checkpoints, transfer packets, project memory, and project-level tool safety settings. For daily work, bind a working folder from the chat page first.

editDialog.title: Edit project
editDialog.desc: Change the project's title and description

empty.title: Nothing Here Yet
empty.desc: Create a new project to launch your first AI automation pipeline.

projects.deleteConfirm: Delete this project? Its stage history will also be removed.

createDialog.title: New Project
createDialog.templateHint: The system will auto-assign the best AI agents to the roles and stages defined by the template.

tokenPlans.title: Usage Monitoring
tokenPlans.desc: Centrally monitor API quota usage across providers. The system will auto-trigger alerts and model fallback strategies based on your plans.

emptyNoProvider.title: No Active Providers
emptyNoProvider.desc: Please configure an API credential in "Providers" first.

tokenPlans.priceIncomplete: {{n}} calls have missing prices, so USD cost may be low

usageMonitor.priceNeverSynced: Remote prices have not been synced yet. Using built-in prices or your manual overrides for now.
usageMonitor.priceVersionSummary: Current price version: {{version}} · enabled {{enabled}} / {{total}} entries · {{history}} historical versions

stats.title: Usage Details
stats.desc: See daily spend, which models cost most, which roles consume the most, and what tools the AI actually ran.
stats.savingsEmpty: No explainable savings yet. Cache hits, smart model picks, and context compression will show up here with details.
stats.savingsExplain: Would have been about {{baseline}}, actually about {{actual}}
stats.savingsTraceSummary: {{traceable}} / {{total}} records trace back to price rows; {{missing}} records are missing price references
stats.savingsCacheDetail: Cache hit {{tokens}} tokens, full input {{input}} / 1M, cache read {{cache}} / 1M
stats.savingsRoutingDetail: Using {{baseline}} would have cost more; this run actually used {{actual}}
stats.savingsCompressionDetail: Before compression about {{before}} tokens, after compression about {{after}} tokens
stats.noData: No usage yet — chat a bit and it'll show up here.
stats.byModelDrilldownHint: Click a model row to see which roles used it
stats.actorRoleEmptyHint: No role usage for this model
stats.perfEmpty: Building samples — after a model has at least 1 call, smart routing starts using real performance with smoothing to avoid small-sample overconfidence.
stats.perfMissingModelLine: Model config was deleted or not found
stats.perfSampleHint: Based on {{n}} real model calls. Call stability means the model call finished normally when the user did not stop it, or Cosmgrid recovered it automatically. It is not cache hit rate or tool success rate.
stats.toolsEmpty: The AI hasn't touched files or commands yet. Once a project has a workspace path, its reads/edits/commands show up here.

settings.title: Preferences
settings.desc: Manage your interface display, language preferences, and core configuration.

smartRouting.title: Smart Routing
smartRouting.toggleDesc: When on, the app picks cheaper, more reliable models based on each model's real track record (call stability / cost / latency), and serves repeated questions from a semantic cache to save tokens. Falls back to rule-based routing when samples are insufficient. Turn off to use basic rule-based routing only.

pureMode.title: Pure Single-Model Mode
pureMode.toggleDesc: When on, it skips the intent judge, background auto-orchestration/model switching, auto debate triggering, semantic cache, project memory retrieval, the self-check retry loop, and failover to backup models on error — leaving only the most basic path: send a message, get a reply from the model you picked, and a real error if it fails (no silent model swap). Use it to check whether single-model chat itself works. Doesn't affect other settings; toggle off anytime.

developerDiagnostics.toggleDesc: When on, the chat work panel shows engineering state for LLM, routing, context, memory/RAG, tools, Skill, Harness, and Workflow. Off by default so normal use only shows task stages and outputs.

cliEngines.title: CLI Engines
cliEngines.desc: Locally spawned Claude Code / Codex CLI use up your subscription quota, not token billing. Which exact snapshot a model alias (e.g. sonnet) resolves to depends on the installed CLI version — check for updates periodically to stay on the latest model without manually tracking version numbers.
cliEngines.none: No Claude CLI / Codex CLI provider configured yet — add one under "Model Providers".
cliEngines.notConfigured: No executable path detected yet — edit this credential under "Model Providers" and click "Detect"

mcp.title: MCP Tool Servers
mcp.desc: Connect external MCP tools to this workspace. Once enabled, the AI can see them in its tool list; each call still asks for confirmation.
mcp.name: Name
mcp.launchConfirmTitle: Allow this local MCP server to start?
mcp.invalidArgs: Args must be a JSON string array, for example ["-y","server-name"].

webSearch.title: Web Search
webSearch.desc: Once configured, the AI can use the web_search tool to search the public web (for when you don't know the exact URL — if you already have a URL, web_fetch handles that without any setup). Backed by Tavily, free tier is about 1000 requests/month.

memoryEmbedding.title: Cross-Project Memory Search
memoryEmbedding.modeDesc: Fast mode: no setup, works immediately, keyword-based, no extra cost. Precise mode: lets AI connect decisions and lessons from your other projects more accurately, but needs an embedding-capable model account configured, and you must click "Update Memory Index" for it to take effect.
memoryEmbedding.credentialPlaceholder: Choose a credential with embedding support
memoryEmbedding.syncDone: Updated the index for {{count}} project memories.
memoryEmbedding.syncFailed: Update failed. Check the API key, Base URL, and embedding model name.
memoryEmbedding.noCredential: No OpenAI / OpenAI-compatible credential is available yet. Add a provider that supports embeddings first to enable precise mode.
memoryEmbedding.remoteHint: After enabling precise mode, click "Update Memory Index" above for it to take effect. It won't auto-update during chat, to avoid slowdowns or extra cost.

projectAssets.title: Project Assets (Advanced)
projectAssets.entryTitle: View legacy projects, checkpoints, and transfer packets
projectAssets.entryDesc: Project assets are no longer a primary entry. Use this only for legacy projects, checkpoints, transfer packets, project memory, and project-level tool safety settings.

localDb.title: Local Database
localDb.desc: All conversations, API credentials, and project data are stored in a local SQLite database.

apiKey.title: API Key Storage
apiKey.desc: API keys are stored in your operating system's secure credential store and are not written to the project database. Linux requires Secret Service, such as GNOME Keyring or KWallet.

commandAllowlist.title: Command Allowlist (AI Execution)
commandAllowlist.desc: Which shell programs AI is allowed to run. Built-in allowlist covers pnpm / npm / git / tsc / python3 / pip3 and other dev-common commands; the field below is your extra allowlist (one program per line, append-only — never shrinks the builtin). Saving triggers a confirmation dialog.
commandAllowlist.builtinNote: Builtin already covers pip3 etc.; double-check before adding. Saving requires confirmation.
commandAllowlist.emptyList: Empty list means no override; AI only runs builtin allowlist.
commandAllowlist.parseError: Existing override failed to parse, reset to empty. Please refill and save.
commandAllowlist.resetSuccess: Reset to default (cleared global + all project-level overrides)
commandAllowlist.resetConfirmBody: Resetting clears global and all project-level overrides, returning to the builtin allowlist. This is irreversible, but you can always add commands again later.

about.title: About CosmGrid
about.tagline: The next-generation multi-agent collaboration workstation, committed to providing the most stable, intuitive, and efficient AI collaboration environment for developers.

addApiKey.title: Add Your First API Key
addApiKey.desc: Go to "Providers", pick a vendor (Anthropic / OpenAI / Google / OpenAI-compatible like GLM/DeepSeek/Qwen), fill in the API Key and your first model.

addSecondModel.title: Add a Second Model (Recommended)
addSecondModel.desc: At least 2 models are needed to showcase multi-AI collaboration — e.g. Opus as the leader, Gemini for coding, Haiku for testing.

browseTemplates.title: Review the Default Roles
browseTemplates.desc: Default Roles include Leader, Architect, Frontend, Backend, Execution Runner, Tester, Reviewer, and Security. You can add your own templates later.

bindWorkspace.title: Return to chat and bind a working folder
bindWorkspace.desc: Most work happens in the chat page. Click "Workspace" beside the input, choose your project folder, and the AI can read files, edit code, and run checks.

tools.confirmTitle: AI requests an action — please confirm
tools.safetyDesc: When the AI runs commands it already has a built-in allowlist (only dev commands like pnpm/npm/git/node) + dangerous-command blocking + your confirmation every time. Add project-specific blocked patterns here (one per line, blocked on match).

recovery.native_resume: Recovered with the CLI's native resume session
recovery.context_replay: Recovered by continuing from the interruption point without repeating earlier content
recovery.fallback_handoff: Primary model stopped; continuing with a fallback model

projectDetail.deleteCheckpoint: Delete this checkpoint? Associated handoff packets will also be removed.
projectDetail.deleteMemory: Delete this memory? This cannot be undone.

createCheckpoint.title: Create Checkpoint
createCheckpoint.selectStage: Pick a stage to generate from
createCheckpoint.generatingHint: AI is drafting the checkpoint from the conversation log…
createCheckpoint.noModelOrCred: This stage has no usable model or credential — AI generation unavailable
createCheckpoint.apiKeyMissing: API Key not found — please re-add the credential
createCheckpoint.titlePlaceholder: e.g. Frontend v1 done, ready for backend integration

placeholders.goal: What this checkpoint aims to achieve
placeholders.currentContext: Key context the next role needs
placeholders.decisions: Why this approach, what was rejected
placeholders.nextSteps: What the next role should do

generateHandoff.title: Generate Handoff
generateHandoff.targetRolePlaceholder: Pick the next role to hand off to
generateHandoff.hint: The handoff packet combines all checkpoint fields into markdown and saves it as a handoff_packets record.

addMemory.title: Add Project Memory
addMemory.titleRequired: Title and content are both required
addMemory.titlePlaceholder: e.g. API Keys stored in the system keychain, not in SQLite
addMemory.contentPlaceholder: What this memory covers / why the decision / future project use
addMemory.tagsHint: Tags help cross-project search match this memory with descriptions / other memories

relatedMemories.title: Cross-Project References
relatedMemories.hint: Only high-importance entries, max 1 per project
